summary: Playwright impersonation routing installs to attacker-namespace sibling package
details: Package is published as `venturo-playwright-runner` but its package.json impersonates Microsoft Playwright: description 'A high-level API to automate web browsers', repository `git+https://github.com/microsoft/playwright.git`, homepage `playwright.dev`, author 'Microsoft Corporation', Apache-2.0. The shipped code mirrors @playwright/test and re-exports the Playwright test API (chromium, firefox, webkit, test, expect, defineConfig). However, package.json declares a runtime dependency on `venturo-playwright-core@1.0.6` rather than the official `playwright-core`, forcing every installer of this package to pull an attacker-namespace sibling into their dependency tree under the guise of a legitimate Playwright install. The tarball itself contains no install hook or obfuscated payload — its function in the supply chain is to legitimize and pull in the sibling, where any malicious code would live. The combination of falsified Microsoft authorship, a verbatim Playwright API surface, and dependency resolution redirected to a typosquat-namespace sibling is the namespace-abuse / dependency-redirect pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
